data_contracts_notes: No schema or data structure changes described. The change is behavioral only.

CONTAINS.problem_statement: MAX/MIN over binary literals returns different results depending on aggregation-elimination path (42 vs 0 with warning 1292)
CONTAINS.root_cause: GROUP BY column rewrite triggers unique-key aggregation-elimination which changes behavior
CONTAINS.solution_approach: Skip aggregation elimination for MAX/MIN with binary literal arguments
CONTAINS.test_coverage: Unit test added; covers both MAX and MIN; tests unique-key and plain GROUP BY cases
CONTAINS.release_note_status: Marked as 'None' (no user-facing release note needed)

MISSING.error_state_detail: Warning 1292 is mentioned as observed, but no specification of when/how it should be handled or suppressed
MISSING.acceptance_criteria: No explicit Given/When/Then; no SQL query examples provided; no explicit test assertion details
MISSING.data_contract: No schema details, no field specifications, no type information
MISSING.boundary_conditions: Unclear what qualifies as a 'binary literal' vs other literals; no threshold or classification rules
MISSING.behavioral_specificity: Vague on 'binary literal' definition; unclear which aggregation paths are affected beyond GROUP BY elimination
MISSING.file_paths: No specific file paths given; only 'pkg/planner' module referenced generically
MISSING.performance_implications: No analysis of performance impact of skipping elimination; unclear if this is a regression or acceptable trade-off
MISSING.query_examples: No concrete SQL examples showing before/after behavior

data_contracts_notes: URI format mentioned (s3:// and azure://) but no schema validation rules, required fields, or error response formats documented

present: Primary goal: enable Azure Blob Storage as metering-storage-uri backend
Problem clearly stated: SDK was pinned to older version, only S3 tested locally
Four concrete changes listed (bump SDK, add config coverage, add constructor coverage, rename variable)
absent: What 'metering' system does or why it matters
How users will consume this feature (configuration steps)
Migration path for existing S3 users
What happens with invalid Azure URIs
What happens with invalid S3 URIs
Boundary conditions (max URI length, special characters)

present: Explicitly scoped: metering SDK bump, S3 + Azure URI config, NewMeter constructor
Explicitly NOT touched: backward compatibility maintained (checked in checklist)
absent: Whether other storage backends are planned
Whether this is feature-complete or phase 1 of broader work
Whether GCS or other providers are in scope

present: Both s3:// and azure:// URIs handled
URI format mentioned: s3://... and azure://...
Azure SDK packages mentioned (azcore, azidentity, storage, etc.)
absent: Specific file paths to modified code
Integration points with existing metering system

present: Two URI schemes supported: s3:// and azure://
